message:
feat: loader options — cacheDir / modelPath / offline + cache mgmt

Extends the Node-side model loader (already does XDG_CACHE_HOME
caching) with the knobs the upcoming CLI needs:

  cacheDir  — alternate cache root (overrides XDG default)
  modelPath — absolute path to a pre-staged model_q4f16.onnx;
              skips both cache and network. Companion .onnx_data
              expected at <modelPath>_data.
  offline   — fail loudly on cache miss (CI / air-gapped)

Each option also picks up a corresponding env var:
  TEXTSIFT_CACHE_DIR / TEXTSIFT_MODEL_PATH / TEXTSIFT_OFFLINE

Plumbed through CreateOptions → Node entry resolver →
NodeBackend → fetchOnnx{Graph,ExtData,TokenizerJson}. Browser path
unchanged (uses OPFS).

Also adds:
- Streaming download progress via onProgress callback (when the
  response has Content-Length + a body stream) so the CLI can show
  a progress bar during the 770 MB first-run download
- getCacheInfo() / clearCache() / getCacheRoot() helpers for the
  CLI's `cache info` / `cache clear` subcommands
- LoaderProgress event type with cache-hit / download-* stages

14 unit tests covering env var precedence, modelPath read, offline
hit/miss, cache info aggregation, and clearCache idempotency.

files.patch:
@@ -211,6 +211,24 @@ export interface CreateOptions {
    */
   signal?: AbortSignal;
 
+  /**
+   * Node-only model loading overrides. Browser ignores these (model
+   * lives in OPFS there). All three are also pickable from env vars:
+   * `TEXTSIFT_CACHE_DIR`, `TEXTSIFT_MODEL_PATH`, `TEXTSIFT_OFFLINE`.
+   *
+   * - `cacheDir` — alternate cache root. Default:
+   *   `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/textsift` or `~/.cache/textsift`.
+   * - `modelPath` — absolute path to a pre-staged
+   *   `model_q4f16.onnx`. Skips both cache and network. The companion
+   *   `.onnx_data` file must live next to it as
+   *   `<modelPath>_data`.
+   * - `offline` — fail loudly on cache miss instead of fetching.
+   *   For CI / air-gapped environments.
+   */
+  cacheDir?: string;
+  modelPath?: string;
+  offline?: boolean;
+
   /**
    * Override the default marker strategy. Default marker for a span
    * labelled `private_person` is `"[private_person]"`. Return `null`
